problems.title: Automation setup is too complex for mid-market teams
problems.description: Support teams struggle to configure routing and workflow automation without external help, which slows time-to-value and increases operational burden.
evidence_quotes: setting up automation has been much harder than expected
We needed outside help to make routing and workflow rules usable
problems.strategic_relevance_reason: This aligns strongly with our strategy of fast onboarding and lower setup complexity for mid-market support teams.

problems.title: Support managers do not fully trust automation decisions
problems.description: Users have low confidence in automated prioritization because the system is hard to understand and explain.
evidence_quotes: our support managers do not always trust the automations
it is difficult to understand why tickets get prioritized a certain way
problems.strategic_relevance_reason: Trust in AI-assisted workflows is a core strategic area for us.

problems.title: Agents lose time due to fragmented context
problems.description: Agents need to check multiple places before replying, which slows issue resolution.
evidence_quotes: Agents also waste time jumping between customer history, internal notes, and billing context before replying
problems.strategic_relevance_reason: This directly affects time-to-resolution and agent productivity.
